title: MPIB: A Benchmark for Medical Prompt Injection Attacks and Clinical Safety in LLMs
abstract_summary: Introduces Medical Prompt Injection Benchmark (MPIB) with 9,697 curated instances evaluating clinical safety under direct and RAG-mediated injection. Proposes Clinical Harm Event Rate (CHER) metric showing attack success rates and actual clinical harm can diverge significantly.

title: Large Reasoning Models Are Autonomous Jailbreak Agents
abstract_summary: Demonstrates that four LRMs (DeepSeek-R1, Gemini 2.5 Flash, Grok 3 Mini, Qwen3 235B) autonomously jailbreak nine target models with 97.14% success rate via multi-turn conversations, documenting alignment regression where reasoning capability enables subversion of other models.

title: Jailbreaking LLMs & VLMs: Mechanisms, Evaluation, and Unified Defenses
authors: Chen, Li, Li, Zhang, Zhang & Hei
abstract_summary: Comprehensive survey proposing a three-dimensional framework for jailbreak attacks (template-based, encoding, ICL manipulation, RL/adversarial, fine-tuning), defenses (prompt obfuscation, output evaluation, model alignment), and evaluation metrics (ASR, toxicity, multimodal accuracy). Distinguishes hallucinations from jailbreaks.

title: Know Thy Enemy: Securing LLMs Against Prompt Injection via Diverse Data Synthesis and Instruction-Level Chain-of-Thought Learning
abstract_summary: Proposes InstruCoT, a three-phase defense combining diverse prompt injection data synthesis with instruction-level chain-of-thought fine-tuning. Achieves over 90% defense rates across seven attack methods on four open-source LLMs while preserving utility.

title: GRP-Obliteration: Unaligning LLMs With a Single Unlabeled Prompt
abstract_summary: Demonstrates that Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), normally used for safety training, can unalign 15 language models using a single unlabeled harmful prompt. The technique generalizes to text-to-image diffusion models, exploiting the reward scoring mechanism to reinforce policy-violating outputs.

title: Prompt Injection is All You Need: A Systemic Framework for Evaluating Healthcare Misinformation in LLMs
abstract_summary: Evaluates 112 attack scenarios across eight LLMs, showing emotional manipulation combined with prompt injection increases dangerous medical misinformation generation from 6.2% baseline to 37.5%. Claude 3.5 Sonnet demonstrates strongest resistance.

title: Efficient Switchable Safety Control in LLMs via Magic-Token-Guided Co-Training
abstract_summary: Proposes unified co-training framework integrating positive, negative, and rejective safety behaviors activated via magic tokens at inference. The 8B model surpasses DeepSeek-R1 (671B) in safety performance while reducing training complexity via distinct Safety Alignment Margin in output space.

title: PromptArmor: Simple yet Effective Prompt Injection Defenses
authors: Shi, Zhu, Wang, Jia, Cai, Liang, Wang, Alzahrani, Lu, Kawaguchi, Alomair, Zhao, Wang, Gong, Guo & Song
abstract_summary: Demonstrates that modern LLMs with advanced reasoning can serve as effective guardrails via careful prompting. Using GPT-4o achieves both false positive and false negative rates below 1% on AgentDojo benchmark, and below 5% on Open Prompt Injection and TensorTrust.

title: Jailbreak Distillation: Renewable Safety Benchmarking
abstract_summary: Proposes JBDistill, a renewable framework that distills jailbreak attacks into reproducible safety benchmarks. Achieves 81.8% effectiveness generalizing to 13 evaluation models including proprietary and reasoning LLMs, enabling fair cross-model comparisons with minimal human effort.

title: Auditing the Gatekeepers: Fuzzing AI Judges to Bypass Security Controls
abstract_summary: Introduces AdvJudge-Zero, an automated fuzzer achieving 99% success in bypassing LLM guardrails across open-weight enterprise LLMs, reward models, and commercial LLMs using stealthy low-perplexity control tokens that flip binary judge decisions. Adversarial training reduces ASR to near zero.

title: System Prompt Poisoning: Persistent Attacks on Large Language Models Beyond User Injection
abstract_summary: Defines system prompt poisoning (SPP) as a persistent attack targeting global system prompts rather than ephemeral user prompts. Proposes Auto-SPP framework with brute-force, adaptive in-context, and adaptive CoT poisoning strategies. Current black-box defenses are ineffective against SPP.

title: Adversary-Aware DPO: Enhancing Safety Alignment in Vision Language Models via Adversarial Training
abstract_summary: Proposes ADPO integrating adversarial training into DPO for Vision Language Models. Uses adversarially-trained reference model and adversarial-aware loss with PGD perturbations in image and latent space. Substantially reduces ASR on LLaVA models across multiple jailbreak attacks.
